questions.stem: What is the primary purpose of the Proton H2 Safety Orientation?

options.text: To introduce employees to Proton H2's executive leadership team and company history
To provide employees and contractors with knowledge of basic Health and Safety Regulations, Responsibilities, and Safe Work Practices that apply to all Proton H2 locations
To fulfill annual performance review requirements for all staff
To outline the company's environmental sustainability targets for the current fiscal year

questions.rationale: As stated in blk_01_03, the Safety Orientation exists to provide employees and contractors with knowledge of basic Health and Safety Regulations, Responsibilities, and Safe Work Practices that apply to all Proton H2 locations.

questions.stem: Which of the following is one of the five key outcomes a worker should be able to achieve by completing this orientation?

options.text: Complete a site-specific equipment certification exam
Access provincial OHS legislations
Memorize all emergency evacuation routes for every Proton H2 location

questions.rationale: According to blk_01_06, one of the five key outcomes is that workers will be able to access provincial OHS legislations upon completing the orientation.

questions.stem: A new contractor has just completed the Proton H2 Safety Orientation. Which action best demonstrates one of the intended outcomes of the orientation?

options.text: The contractor locates Proton H2's Health and Safety Policy and references its content before beginning work
The contractor skips reviewing the safety manual because they have prior industry experience
The contractor waits for a supervisor to explain all safety rules before starting any task
The contractor assumes their previous employer's safe work practices apply at all Proton H2 locations

questions.rationale: blk_01_06 lists 'Locate Proton H2's Health and Safety Policy, and be able to reference its content' as the first key outcome of the orientation, making opt_a the correct demonstration of an intended outcome.

questions.stem: According to Proton H2's health and safety expectations, who is accountable for health and safety performance, and what must be done when an accident, incident, or near miss occurs?

options.text: Only supervisors are accountable; incidents should be reported within 24 hours
Everyone is accountable; all accidents, incidents, and near misses must be reported immediately
Only management is accountable; minor incidents do not need to be reported
Workers are accountable; incidents should be reported at the end of the shift

questions.rationale: blk_02_03 states everyone is accountable for their health and safety performance, and blk_02_04 states all accidents, incidents, and near misses must be reported immediately.

questions.stem: Near misses do not need to be reported as long as no one was injured.

options.text: True
False

questions.rationale: blk_02_04 explicitly states that all accidents, incidents, AND near misses must be reported immediately; there is no exemption for near misses that did not result in injury.

questions.stem: Where can the Proton H2 Health, Safety and Environmental (HSE) Policy be found?

options.text: At the end of the Health and Safety Program Manual
On the Proton H2 company intranet homepage only
At the beginning of the Health and Safety Program Manual
In the provincial OH&S legislation database

questions.rationale: blk_02_09 states that the Proton H2 HSE Policy can be found at the beginning of the Health and Safety Program Manual.

questions.stem: A worker is unsure about specific content in the Proton H2 Health and Safety Program Manual. What should they do?

options.text: Ignore the unclear section and proceed with work as they understand it
Ask questions if they are not sure about the content or other information in the safety program
Wait until the next scheduled safety meeting to raise the concern

questions.rationale: blk_02_11 explicitly states workers should ask questions if they are not sure about content or other information in the safety program.

questions.stem: Which of the following correctly lists all five types of hazard controls used in Proton H2 risk assessments?

options.text: Elimination, substitution, engineering controls, administrative controls, and PPE
Elimination, substitution, training, signage, and PPE
Engineering controls, administrative controls, PPE, emergency response, and incident reporting
Inspection, elimination, substitution, supervision, and PPE

questions.rationale: blk_03_02 identifies the five hazard control types as: eliminating the hazard, substituting to lessen or eliminate it, engineering controls, administrative controls, and PPE.

questions.stem: A worksite assessment reveals a chemical hazard. The safety team decides to replace the hazardous chemical with a less dangerous substance. Which type of hazard control is being applied?

options.text: Administrative control
Personal protective equipment (PPE)
Substitution
Engineering control (enclosure)

questions.rationale: blk_03_02 defines substitution as replacing something to lessen or eliminate an identified hazard, which matches the scenario of replacing a hazardous chemical with a safer alternative.

questions.stem: Under provincial OH&S legislation, what is the employer's primary responsibility regarding workers?

options.text: To ensure workers are paid fair wages and receive adequate rest breaks
To do everything practicable to ensure the health, safety and welfare of all workers, and ensure they are aware of their rights and duties
To provide workers with optional safety training programs each quarter
To report all incidents directly to the provincial government within 48 hours

questions.rationale: blk_04_02 states that provincial legislation requires owners/employers to do everything practicable to ensure the health, safety and welfare of all workers and to ensure workers are aware of their rights and duties.

questions.stem: A Proton H2 worker notices that a nearby contractor is performing work in a manner that could endanger other workers in the area. According to OH&S legislation, what should the worker do?

options.text: Ignore the situation since the contractor is not a direct Proton H2 employee
Take reasonable care and attention to protect the health and safety of all workers in the vicinity of work being done by Proton H2 and its contractors
Document the issue and report it only at the next scheduled management meeting

questions.rationale: blk_04_03 states workers must take reasonable care and attention to protect the health and safety of all workers and others in or in the vicinity of work being done by Proton H2 and its contractors.

questions.stem: In the event of an emergency at a Proton H2 worksite, which number should be contacted?

options.text: (403) 000 0000 — the head office telephone number
1 (877) 000 0000 — the 24-hour emergency number
The local municipal non-emergency line
The provincial OH&S legislation hotline

questions.rationale: blk_05_03 and blk_05_02 both specify that in the event of an emergency, workers must contact the 24-hour emergency number: 1 (877) 000 0000.
